examples.input: Tom makes toast for breakfast every morning. It takes 2 minutes to toast the first side of a slice and 1 minute to toast the second side. His toaster can hold two slices at a time. If Tom wants to eat 3 slices of toast, what is the minimum time required?
Sam's father is frying eggs for him. The pan can hold two eggs at a time. It takes 2 minutes to cook an egg (1 minute per side). What is the minimum time required for his father to fry 3 eggs?

Lily gets up in the morning and has several tasks to complete. Getting dressed takes 3 minutes, brushing her teeth and washing her face takes 4 minutes, boiling water takes 15 minutes, eating breakfast takes 7 minutes, washing dishes takes 2 minutes, packing her schoolbag takes 2 minutes, and preparing milk (to take to school) takes 1 minute. What is the minimum time required to complete all these tasks?

Mrs. Miller has 9 stools that require two coats of paint. A painter takes 2 minutes to apply a coat to one stool. However, the first coat must dry for 15 minutes before the second coat can be applied. What is the minimum time required to finish painting all 9 stools?

Mark wakes up in the morning and has several tasks to complete. Brushing his teeth and washing his face (using cold water) takes 5 minutes. Rinsing the kettle takes 1 minute, while boiling water takes 10 minutes. Packing his schoolbag takes 3 minutes, preparing milk takes 1 minute, and eating breakfast takes 10 minutes. If Mark organizes his schedule efficiently, what is the minimum time required?

Sarah has several tasks to complete in the morning: brushing her teeth and washing her face takes 4 minutes, eating breakfast takes 8 minutes, rinsing the kettle takes 1 minute, boiling water takes 12 minutes, and filling a thermos takes 2 minutes. What is the minimum time required for Sarah to complete all these tasks?

A construction project must be completed within a specific deadline. If Team A works alone, they finish exactly on schedule. If Team B works alone, they would take 3 days longer than the deadline. If both teams work together for 2 days and then Team B continues alone, the project is finished exactly on schedule. How many days is the deadline?

A farm planned to harvest its wheat with 18 workers, working 6 hours a day for 15 days. To speed up the process, the farm later increased the workforce by 9 workers per day and extended the daily working time by 2 hours. By how many days was the harvest completed earlier than originally scheduled?

A mining operation planned to complete an excavation project in 30 days, employing 50 workers for 10 hours a day. To expedite the process, the workforce was increased by 25 workers, and the daily shift was extended by 4 hours. How many days ahead of schedule was the project actually completed? (Round to one decimal place)

Adam, Ben, Charlie, and Daniel arrive simultaneously at a single water tap. Since only one person can use the tap at a time, the others must wait. Adam needs 3 minutes to wash a mop, Ben needs 2 minutes to wash a cleaning cloth, Charlie needs 1 minute to fill a bucket, and Daniel needs 10 minutes to wash clothes. What is the minimum total time spent by the group (including both usage and waiting time)?

Luke needs to herd four cows—A, B, C, and D—across a river. It takes Cow A 1 minute to cross, Cow B 2 minutes, Cow C 5 minutes, and Cow D 6 minutes. Luke can take two cows across at a time (riding one and leading the other). What is the minimum time required to get all four cows to the opposite bank?

Tom lives 10 km from his school. If he starts cycling at 8:00 AM at a speed of 20 km/h, he arrives exactly on time, which is 30 minutes faster than if he were to run the entire distance. Today, he departed at 8:00 AM but caught a ride with a neighbor traveling at 40 km/h. He ran the remaining 2 km. How many minutes early will he arrive today?

Adam, Ben, and Charlie arrive simultaneously at a hot water tap carrying 2, 3, and 1 thermos bottles, respectively. There is only one tap available. Assuming it takes 1 minute to fill a bottle, what is the minimum total time spent by the three of them (including both filling and waiting time)?

The General Manager called Alex, Brian, and Carl to the office for individual meetings. Alex's meeting requires 5 minutes, Brian's requires 7 minutes, and Carl's requires 6 minutes. What is the minimum total time spent by the three of them, including both meeting and waiting time?

Three customers—Arthur, Bill, and Chris—arrive at a counter simultaneously to make purchases. Arthur requires 5 minutes to be served, Bill requires 3 minutes, and Chris requires 6 minutes. What is the minimum total time spent by the three of them (including both service and waiting time)?

Pancakes need to be cooked on both sides. It takes 4 minutes to cook the first side and 2 minutes to cook the second side. The pan can hold three pancakes at a time. What is the minimum time required to fully cook 5 pancakes?

A construction project is scheduled to last 45 days. If the work were performed entirely by an excavator, it would be finished 15 days ahead of schedule. However, if performed entirely by manual labor, it would be delayed by 15 days. The daily cost is $200 for an excavator and $80 for manual labor. To complete the project within the 45-day deadline at the minimum cost, for how many days must the excavator be used?

A road construction project must be completed within a 60-day deadline. If only heavy machinery is used, the project can be finished 20 days ahead of schedule at a daily cost of 300 dollars. If only manual labor is used, the project will be delayed by 20 days at a daily cost of 100 dollars. How many days of heavy machinery use will minimize costs while completing the project on time?

A company is required to complete a market research project within a 10-day deadline. There are two options: employing a professional market research team or using the company's internal staff. If the professional team handles the entire project, they can finish 5 days ahead of schedule. However, if the internal staff does the work alone, the project will be delayed by 10 days. If the professional team is utilized for only the first 4 days, how many days ahead of schedule will the project be completed?

Lisa usually travels to the park at a speed of 10 km/h when skateboarding and 8 km/h when running. Today, she left early. To avoid arriving too soon, she decided to alternate between skateboarding and running without taking any breaks, arriving exactly 13 minutes later. Given that the distance to the park is 2 km, for how many minutes did she spend skateboarding?

Emily has 9 hours available today. She needs to spend 2 hours doing housework, 4 hours reading a classic novel, 1 hour cooking rice in a rice cooker, and 2 hours listening to a radio drama. What is the maximum amount of time she can spend resting?
This morning, it took me 4 minutes to boil water, 2 minutes to wash my face, and 3 minutes to brush my teeth. What is the minimum time required to complete all these tasks?

Guests have arrived at your home, and you plan to prepare tea for them. Rinsing the kettle takes 2 minutes, boiling the water takes 12 minutes, buying tea leaves takes 8 minutes, washing the teacups takes 2 minutes, and brewing the tea takes 1 minute. What is the minimum time required to serve the tea?

Suppose there are 8 hours available on Saturday. Helping your mother clean the house takes 2 hours, working out takes 1 hour, and going to the movies takes 2 hours. What is the maximum amount of time remaining for rest?

Every evening, Lily has several tasks to complete: memorizing vocabulary words takes 6 minutes, boiling water takes 10 minutes, preparing milk takes 2 minutes, and drinking it takes 5 minutes. What is the minimum time required to finish all these tasks?

On Sunday, Tom helps his mother with several chores. Sweeping the floor takes 10 minutes, wiping the furniture takes 15 minutes, washing clothes in the washing machine takes 40 minutes, and hanging out the laundry takes 5 minutes. If he organizes his schedule efficiently, what is the minimum time required to complete all these tasks?

Fiona is preparing a cup of hot chocolate. Washing the cup and the spoon takes 1 minute each. Boiling the water takes 8 minutes, and getting the cocoa mix takes 2 minutes. If Fiona organizes her tasks efficiently, what is the minimum time required before she can enjoy her drink?

Three students—Adam, Ben, and Carl—arrive together to wash their hands. Adam needs 1 minute, Ben needs 3 minutes, and Carl needs 2 minutes. There is only one water tap available. If they arrange the order to minimize the total waiting time, what is the sum of the waiting times for the three students?
A frying pan can hold two fish at a time. It takes 6 minutes to cook a fish (3 minutes per side). What is the minimum time required to cook 3 fish?

On Sunday, Harry wants to help his mother with housework. Washing clothes in the washing machine takes 20 minutes, sweeping the floor takes 6 minutes, wiping the furniture takes 15 minutes, and hanging out the laundry takes 5 minutes. Please help Harry determine the minimum time required to complete these tasks.

Linda is about to go out. Before leaving, she needs to complete the following four tasks: tidying the room (5 minutes), loading clothes and water into the washing machine (1 minute), running the automatic washing cycle (12 minutes), and polishing her shoes (3 minutes). What is the minimum number of minutes required to complete these four tasks?

During a school cleanup, four students—Aaron, Blake, Colin, and Derek—need to fill their buckets at a single water tap. Filling Aaron's bucket takes 3 minutes, Blake's takes 5 minutes, Colin's takes 2 minutes, and Derek's takes 4 minutes. What is the minimum total waiting time for the four students?

Every morning, George has three tasks to complete after getting up: listening to the news (20 minutes), washing up (8 minutes), and eating breakfast (15 minutes). What is the minimum number of minutes required for him to finish these tasks?

Rose is using a pan to cook pancakes. The pan can hold up to two pancakes at a time. Each pancake needs to be cooked on both sides, taking 2 minutes per side. What is the minimum time required for her to cook 5 pancakes?

Mike's mother is ill, and he needs to attend to her before heading to school. The tasks and their required times are: taking her temperature (5 minutes), finding cold medicine (3 minutes), pouring water and letting it cool (8 minutes), and giving her the medicine (1 minute). If Mike organizes his schedule efficiently, what is the minimum time required?

On Sunday, Helen was learning to do housework at home. Sweeping the floor takes 5 minutes, mopping takes 10 minutes, wiping the tables and chairs takes 5 minutes, washing clothes in the washing machine takes 35 minutes, tidying the room takes 8 minutes, and hanging out the laundry takes 5 minutes. What is the minimum time Helen needs to complete these tasks?

Jack is making tea for a guest. Rinsing the kettle takes 2 minutes, boiling the water takes 12 minutes, washing the teacups (using cold water) takes 2 minutes, and getting the tea leaves takes 1 minute. To serve the tea as quickly as possible, what is the minimum time required if he organizes the tasks efficiently?
A waffle maker can bake two waffles at a time. Both sides of each waffle must be baked, and it takes 3 minutes to bake one side. What is the minimum time required to bake 5 waffles?

Mike is preparing tea for a guest. The tasks require the following amounts of time: filling the kettle (1 minute), boiling the water (6 minutes), washing the teacups (using cold water) (2 minutes), getting the tea leaves (1 minute), and brewing the tea (1 minute). If Mike schedules these tasks efficiently, what is the minimum time required to serve the tea?
A frying pan can fry at most two sea bass at a time. It takes 4 minutes to cook one sea bass (2 minutes per side). If Mom wants to fry 3 sea bass, what is the minimum time required?

Gary is herding horses across a river while riding on horseback. There are four horses: Adam, Bill, Carl, and Dave. It takes Adam 2 minutes to cross, Bill 3 minutes, Carl 6 minutes, and Dave 7 minutes. Only two horses can cross at the same time. What is the minimum time required to get all four horses to the opposite bank?

Kevin wants to organize his schedule efficiently: (1) Making the bed (3 minutes), (2) Brushing teeth (3 minutes), (3) Washing face (2 minutes), and (4) Listening to a podcast (8 minutes). What is the minimum time required for Kevin to complete these tasks?

Peter needs to photocopy 7 documents. Both sides of each document need to be copied. If the photocopier can only copy one side at a time and can handle a maximum of 2 sheets at once, what is the minimum number of times the machine must be operated to finish the job?

Paul, Quinn, and Robert plan to borrow the book "Harry Potter" from the library, but there is only one copy available. Paul says he can finish reading it in a week, Quinn says she needs half a month, and Robert says he can finish it in 5 days. How should the borrowing order be arranged to minimize the total waiting time for all three?

Three ships are waiting to be unloaded at a dock. Ship A requires 10 hours to unload, Ship B requires 12 hours, and Ship C requires 9 hours. Due to a shortage of manpower, the ships must be unloaded one at a time. What is the optimal unloading order to minimize the total waiting time for all three ships?
examples.target: Ship C -> Ship A -> Ship B

examples.input: Third-grade students are visiting a science exhibition and queuing for ticket inspection. Alice is 39 places ahead of Betty, and she is 50 places behind Charlie. David is 78 places ahead of Betty. Among Alice, David, and Charlie, who is at the very front of the line?

Emma wants to help her mom with the following chores on Sunday: using the washing machine (25 minutes), sweeping the floor (6 minutes), dusting the furniture (10 minutes), hanging the laundry (5 minutes), and taking out the trash (3 minutes). If she schedules her time efficiently, what is the minimum time required to complete all these tasks?
A game takes 5 minutes per round and can be played by either one or two players. Frank, George, and Harry each want to play two rounds. What is the minimum time required?

On Sunday morning, Ivy plans to complete the following tasks: sweeping the floor (3 minutes), mopping the floor (12 minutes), boiling water (8 minutes), and packing her schoolbag (2 minutes). To finish the work as early as possible, what is the minimum time required?

To cook pancakes on a stove, both sides must be cooked. It takes 3 minutes to cook one side. The stove can only hold 2 pancakes at a time. If 3 pancakes need to be cooked, what is the minimum time required?

Jack takes 5 minutes to get dressed in the morning, 3 minutes to make the bed, 2 minutes to brush his teeth, 1 minute to wash his face, 3 minutes to warm up milk, 2 minutes to toast bread, and 8 minutes to eat breakfast. What is the minimum time required to complete everything?

Guests have arrived at Liam's house, and he wants to make herbal tea for them. The tasks require the following time: washing the kettle (2 minutes), boiling the water (15 minutes), washing the teacups (using cold water) (1 minute), filling the kettle (1 minute), getting the tea leaves (1 minute), and brewing the tea (1 minute). What is the minimum time required for Liam to serve the tea?

Mom is using a small frying pan to fry pancakes. The pan can hold at most two pancakes at a time. If it takes 2 minutes to fry one pancake (1 minute per side), what is the minimum time required to fry 7 pancakes?

When toasting bread, the first side takes 2 minutes. The second side takes only 1 minute because the bread dries out. Mom's pan can hold two slices at a time. What is the minimum time required to toast 3 slices?

Three customers enter a barbershop at the same time. Adam wants a haircut and a shave, but no blow-dry. Bill only wants a shave, not a haircut. Carl wants a haircut, a blow-dry, and a shave. There is only one barber available. Please arrange a reasonable order of service to minimize the total waiting time for all three.
In a steakhouse, it takes 6 minutes to grill a steak (3 minutes per side). If the griddle can hold a maximum of 4 steaks at a time, what is the minimum time required to grill 15 steaks?

The school copy center needs to photocopy 32 documents, and both sides of each document must be copied. If a maximum of 3 sheets can be placed in the machine at one time, what is the minimum number of times the machine needs to be operated?

In a pork chop restaurant, it takes 6 minutes to grill a pork chop (3 minutes per side). If the griddle can hold a maximum of 4 chops at a time, what is the minimum time required to grill 20 pork chops?

Every morning after waking up, Danny has the following tasks to do: getting dressed (2 minutes), making the bed (2 minutes), washing his face and combing his hair (5 minutes), using the bathroom (3 minutes), waiting for breakfast to cool down (12 minutes), and eating breakfast (8 minutes). What is the minimum time required to complete these tasks?

Four students are lining up to fill water buckets of different sizes. It takes Alex 5 minutes to fill his bucket, Ben 4 minutes, Chris 3 minutes, and Dan 2 minutes. There is only one faucet available. What is the minimum total waiting time for all the students?

Mr. Evans needs to have a talk with four students: Adam, Brett, Cody, and Drew. The conversation will take 6 minutes with Adam, 10 minutes with Brett, 8 minutes with Cody, and 5 minutes with Drew. What is the minimum total time the students will spend waiting?

Before going shopping, Sarah needs to finish the following chores: tidying up the room (5 minutes), loading the washing machine (1 minute), running the wash cycle (12 minutes), polishing her shoes (3 minutes), and hanging out the laundry (1 minute). What is the soonest she can leave?

With her mother at work, Lisa needs to prepare a meal for herself. Rinsing the rice takes 3 minutes, washing the pot takes 1 minute, and cooking the rice in a rice cooker takes 20 minutes. Heating up pre-cooked dishes in the microwave takes 8 minutes, and preparing instant soup takes 3 minutes. What is the minimum time required before Lisa can eat?

When using a toaster, the first side of a slice takes 2 minutes to toast, and the second side takes only 1 minute, meaning it takes 3 minutes to toast one slice completely. Ken's toaster can hold a maximum of 2 slices at a time. If he needs to toast 3 slices for breakfast, what is the minimum time required?

Five people—Adam, Brian, Charles, David, and Edward—are waiting at a single bank window to make deposits or withdrawals. The time required for their transactions is as follows: Adam takes 10 minutes, Brian 6 minutes, Charles 8 minutes, David 3 minutes, and Edward 15 minutes. What is the minimum total waiting time for all five people?

On Sunday, Cindy helped her mom cook rice in a rice cooker, which took 35 minutes. She spent 15 minutes doing her literature homework and 10 minutes doing her math homework. If Cindy schedules her time efficiently, what is the minimum time required to complete these tasks?

A doctor is seeing three patients: Helen, Mark, and James. Helen's consultation takes 4 minutes, James's takes 10 minutes, and Mark's takes 7 minutes. What is the minimum total waiting time for the three patients?

Small blackboards need to be painted on both sides. Painting one side takes 2 minutes, but the paint must dry for 10 minutes before the other side can be painted. What is the minimum time required to finish painting 6 blackboards?

A barbershop has only one barber. Four customers arrived at the same time. Based on the haircuts they require, Alan needs 15 minutes, Bob needs 25 minutes, Chris needs 18 minutes, and Dan needs 40 minutes. In what order should the barber serve them to minimize the total time spent on haircuts and waiting for all four customers?

Sam has a cold and wants to rest as soon as he finishes taking his medicine. It takes 1 minute to pour boiling water, 7 minutes for the water to cool, 1 minute to find the medicine, and 5 minutes to take his temperature. If Sam arranges these steps efficiently, what is the minimum time required before he can rest?
A pan can hold two slices of bread at a time. It takes 2 minutes to toast each side. If Rachel wants to eat 3 slices for breakfast, what is the minimum time she needs to wait?

Three students are at the school library to return books, but only one person can be served at a time. It takes Arthur 4 minutes to return his books, Ben 2 minutes, and Charlie 8 minutes. What is the minimum total time the group spends waiting?

Laura, Will, and Vanessa are competing in a speech contest to become a student reporter. Laura's speech takes 6 minutes, Will's takes 2 minutes, and Vanessa's takes 5 minutes. In what order should they speak to minimize the total waiting time for the group?

Team A can repair a canal in 20 days working alone, while Team B requires 30 days. When the two teams work together, their efficiency decreases due to coordination challenges: Team A operates at four-fifths of its normal rate, and Team B operates at nine-tenths of its normal rate. The project is scheduled to be completed within 16 days. If the goal is to minimize the number of days the teams work together, for how many days must they collaborate?

A job is expected to take 4 hours if Adam and Ben work together, and 5 hours if Ben and Carl work together. The current plan is for Adam and Carl to work together for the first 2 hours, leaving Ben to finish the remaining work in 6 hours. Based on this information, how many hours would it take for Ben to complete the entire job alone?

A project is being planned with two different schedules for alternating daily shifts. If Adam works on the first day, Ben on the second, Adam on the third, and so on, the project will be completed in exactly a whole number of days. If Ben works on the first day, Adam on the second, Ben on the third, and so on, it will take half a day longer to complete than the first schedule. Given that Ben needs 17 days to complete the project alone, how many days would it take for Adam to complete it alone?

According to the production plan, a senior mechanic and an apprentice are assigned to process the same number of parts. When the senior mechanic has completed half of the work, the apprentice has finished 120 parts. When the senior mechanic completes the entire task, the apprentice has finished 4/5 of the task. How many parts are in the batch?

A batch of saplings needs to be planted. If the task is shared by both men and women, the average is 6 trees per person. If the task is assigned only to the women, the average is 10 trees per person. If the task is assigned only to the men, how many trees will each person plant on average?

Walter plans to go out by bicycle, but he must finish several chores first: pumping up the bicycle tires (2 minutes), tidying up his dorm room (7 minutes), polishing his shoes (2 minutes), filling the machine with water and loading the clothes (1 minute), running the automatic wash cycle (12 minutes), and finally rinsing, wringing out, and hanging the clothes (5 minutes). What is the minimum time required for Walter to complete everything?

Violet gets up in the morning. Making the bed takes 3 minutes, washing up takes 4 minutes, boiling water takes 10 minutes, eating breakfast takes 7 minutes, washing the dishes takes 1 minute, and packing her schoolbag takes 2 minutes. What is the minimum time required to complete these tasks?

Mark leaves home at 6:50 AM every morning and arrives at school at 7:20 AM. His teacher suggests that he arrive 6 minutes earlier tomorrow. If he leaves at 6:50 AM as usual, he must increase his speed by 25 meters per minute to arrive at the suggested time. What is the distance from Mark's home to the school?

A project needs to be completed by a specific deadline. If Team A works alone, they will finish 1 day past the deadline. If Team B works alone, they will finish 4 days past the deadline. Under the current plan, both teams work together for 3 days, and then Team B completes the remaining work alone, finishing exactly on the deadline. How many days is the deadline?

Martin and Steven are counting a batch of books. It takes Martin the same amount of time to count 200 books as it takes Steven to count 300 books. Given that Steven counts 10 more books per minute than Martin, how many books does Martin count per minute on average?

A tunnel is being constructed on Interstate 70. If Team A works alone, the project is projected to take 200 days to complete. To open the highway sooner, Team A and Team B worked simultaneously, finishing the job in 120 days. How many days would it take for Team B to complete the work alone?

A batch of books needs to be organized within a set timeframe. If one person performed the task alone, it would take 60 hours. The plan calls for a group of people to work for 1 hour, followed by 15 additional people joining them for another 2 hours to finish the job exactly. Assuming everyone works at the same rate, how many people were in the first group?

Lily is preparing tea for a guest. Filling the kettle takes 1 minute, boiling the water takes 5 minutes, washing the teacups (using cold water) takes 3 minutes, and getting the tea leaves takes 1 minute. If Lily organizes her time efficiently, what is the minimum time required to complete these tasks?
Ruby is making pancakes using a frying pan that can hold two at a time. It takes 2 minutes to cook one side, and both sides need to be cooked. What is the minimum time required to cook 3 pancakes?

Every morning after arriving at school, Mr. Scott has to complete the following tasks: arranging desks and chairs (5 minutes), cleaning the office (10 minutes), listening to music (30 minutes), grading yesterday's homework (20 minutes), doing morning exercises (10 minutes), and meeting with the Class President (5 minutes). If class starts at 8:00 AM, what is the latest time Mr. Scott can arrive at school?

A baker is toasting bread. Toasting the first side takes 2 minutes, and the second side takes 1 minute, meaning it takes 3 minutes to toast one slice completely. The toasting rack can hold two slices at a time. If he needs to toast 5 slices for his family's breakfast, what is the minimum time required?
Cooking a pancake requires frying both sides. Each side takes 3 minutes. The frying pan can hold 3 pancakes at a time. What is the minimum time required to cook 5 pancakes?

It takes 20 hours for one person to repair a batch of parts alone. Initially, one person works for 2 hours. Then, a group of additional workers joins him, and they work together for another 3 hours to complete the repair work. Assuming everyone works at the same rate, how many workers were added?

A school intends to hire workers to repair the lawn. If one person performed the task alone, it would take 240 hours. The plan is to have an initial group work for 5 hours, followed by 15 additional workers joining them for another 4 hours to complete the job. Assuming everyone works at the same rate, how many workers were in the first group?

Adam and Ben are making the same type of toy. It takes Adam the same amount of time to make 90 toys as it takes Ben to make 120 toys. Given that they make a total of 35 toys per day together, how many toys does Adam make per day?

To repair a highway, a 1,300-meter tunnel needs to be excavated. To improve efficiency, the construction management team decided to have Team A and Team B start excavating simultaneously from opposite ends. It is known that Team A excavates 10 meters more per day than Team B. Additionally, it takes Team A the same amount of time to excavate 300 meters as it takes Team B to excavate 200 meters. How many meters can each team excavate per day?
examples.target: Team A: 30 meters per day; Team B: 20 meters per day

examples.input: Two engineering teams, Team A and Team B, are bidding for a city beautification project. Estimates show that Team A would take 60 days to complete the project alone, while Team B would take 90 days. If Team A works alone for the first 20 days and the remaining work is completed by both teams working together, how many days will they collaborate to finish the project?

Ms. Lewis is looking to have a small program developed. It takes Leo 6 hours to complete the task independently, while Will takes 8 hours. If they work together, how many hours will it take to complete the job? (Round to one decimal place).

Washing dishes and tidying up the kitchen takes 20 minutes. Boiling water and brewing tea takes 15 minutes. Washing a duvet cover in the washing machine takes 1 hour. Hand-washing clothes takes 20 minutes, and spin-drying the clothes takes 5 minutes. What is the minimum time required for Dylan's mom to complete all these tasks?
It takes 8 minutes to boil an egg. A pot can hold up to 15 eggs at a time. What is the minimum time required to boil 27 eggs?

Miles is preparing milk tea for a guest. Washing the kettle takes 2 minutes, boiling the water takes 12 minutes, washing the cups (using cold water) takes 2 minutes, and getting the milk tea powder takes 1 minute. To serve the guest as early as possible, what is the minimum time required before Miles can start making the milk tea?

Before going to sleep, James needs to do the following: getting the bed ready (2 minutes), washing his face and brushing his teeth (3 minutes), packing his schoolbag (2 minutes), listening to a podcast (5 minutes), and washing his feet (5 minutes). What is the minimum time required for James to complete these tasks?

Mom is toasting bread slices. Each slice takes 5 minutes to toast: 3 minutes on one side and 2 minutes on the other. The oven can hold two slices at a time. If Mom needs to toast 3 slices, is 8 minutes enough?

An amusement park has an archery range and a cycling track. It takes 4 minutes to complete a round of either activity, and only one child can use a facility at a time. Three children are visiting, and they all want to play both games. What is the minimum time required for everyone to finish?

Mrs. Taylor's frying pan can hold a maximum of 10 potato pancakes at a time. Both sides need to be fried, and it takes 3 minutes per side. A customer has ordered 15 pancakes and requested that they be ready within 10 minutes. Can Mrs. Taylor complete the order within the requested time?

Three students—Leo, Maya, and Nina—need to take two tests: Recitation and Handwriting. Each test takes 5 minutes per student, and the tests are administered by two different teachers. What is the minimum time required for everyone to complete both tests?

Wendy gets up in the morning. It takes her 3 minutes to brush her teeth and wash her face, 15 minutes to boil water, 2 minutes to fill the water bottle, and 8 minutes to eat breakfast. What is the minimum time required for Wendy to complete these tasks?

Luke gets up in the morning and has several things to do: washing the kettle (1 minute), boiling water (12 minutes), filling the water bottle (2 minutes), eating breakfast (8 minutes), and packing his schoolbag (2 minutes). What is the minimum time required to get everything done?

Four people—Adam, Ben, Carl, and Dan—need to cross a bridge. It takes them 1, 2, 5, and 10 minutes to cross, respectively. Because it is dark, they must use a flashlight, but they only have one. The bridge has a weight limit and can only support two people at a time. What is the minimum time required for everyone to get across?

Alex, Brian, and Carl arrive simultaneously at a water tap that can only be used by one person at a time. It takes Alex 3 minutes to wash a mop, Brian 10 minutes to wash clothes, and Carl 2 minutes to fill a bucket. What is the minimum total time spent by all three people combined (the sum of each person's usage and waiting time)?

Zack, Quinn, and Steve arrive simultaneously at a small restaurant. Since there is only one chef, meals must be prepared one at a time. Quinn orders dumplings which take 6 minutes, Zack orders a fried egg which takes 2 minutes, and Steve orders noodles which take 5 minutes. What is the minimum total waiting time for the three of them combined?

In the morning, Lucy needs 3 minutes to brush her teeth and wash her face, 1 minute to wash the kettle, 8 minutes to read, 18 minutes to boil water, 2 minutes to fill the thermos, and 5 minutes to eat breakfast. What is the minimum time required for her to complete all these tasks?
A frying pan can hold two pancakes at a time. It takes 4 minutes to fry one pancake (2 minutes per side). If three pancakes need to be fried, what is the minimum time required?

Sarah is going out with her mother. Before leaving, they need to complete the following chores: tidying the room (5 minutes), running the washing machine (20 minutes), hanging out the laundry (2 minutes), polishing shoes (4 minutes), washing faces and brushing hair (8 minutes), and packing bags (2 minutes). If the tasks are arranged efficiently, what is the minimum time needed before they can leave?

Three students go to the library to borrow books. It takes Arthur 4 minutes, Ben 8 minutes, and Charles 3 minutes. What is the minimum total time spent by all of them (the sum of each person's borrowing and waiting time)?

On Sunday, several colleagues of Grace's mother came to visit. Her mother asked Grace to boil water and make tea for the guests. The process involves the following tasks: washing the kettle (1 minute), boiling the water (8 minutes), washing the teapot (1 minute, without using hot water), washing the teacups (2 minutes, without using hot water), fetching the tea leaves (2 minutes), and brewing the tea (1 minute). What is the minimum time required for Grace to serve the tea?

Harry has several tasks to complete in the morning: washing the kettle (1 minute), boiling water (14 minutes), filling the thermos (2 minutes), eating breakfast (8 minutes), and packing his bag (2 minutes). What is the minimum time required to complete all these tasks?

In the morning, Ivy has the following tasks: brushing her teeth and washing her face (4 minutes, without using hot water), reading (8 minutes), boiling water (10 minutes), preparing milk (1 minute), and eating breakfast (5 minutes). What is the minimum time required before she can leave for school?

Jenny is getting ready to go out. Before she leaves, she needs to finish the following chores: tidying the room (5 minutes), putting clothes into the washing machine (1 minute), running the automatic wash cycle (12 minutes), polishing shoes (3 minutes), and hanging out the laundry (4 minutes). What is the minimum time required before she can set off?

When cooking pancakes, the first side takes 3 minutes and the second side takes 2 minutes. The pan can hold a maximum of two pancakes at a time. What is the minimum time required to cook three pancakes?

Kyle, Liam, Mason, and Noah each own a wheat field, but there is only one combine harvester available for them to use. Harvesting Kyle's field takes 4 hours, Liam's takes 1 hour, Mason's takes 3 hours, and Noah's takes 2 hours. What is the minimum total time spent by all four farmers combined (including both harvesting and waiting times)?

Four students are waiting for treatment in the infirmary. There is only one doctor, so only one student can be treated at a time. Adam needs to get a shot, which takes 3 minutes; Ben needs to change a bandage, which takes 4 minutes; Carl needs to apply antiseptic, which takes 2 minutes; and Dan needs to use eye drops, which takes 1 minute. What is the minimum total waiting time for all of them combined?

Three customers go to a counter to make purchases. Since only one person can be served at a time, Steve needs 4 minutes, Tony needs 6 minutes, and Vince needs 2 minutes. What is the minimum total time spent by the three of them (the sum of their respective transaction and waiting times)?

George is crossing a river on horseback. He has four horses: Alex, Brian, Charlie, and David. It takes Alex 7 minutes, Brian 2 minutes, Charlie 3 minutes, and David 8 minutes to cross. If only two horses can cross at a time, what is the minimum number of minutes required to get all four horses to the opposite bank?

Three students, Linda, Wendy, and Henry, arrive simultaneously at the school infirmary for treatment. Linda needs to get a shot, which takes 5 minutes; Wendy needs to have a bandage applied, which takes 3 minutes; and Henry needs to use eye drops, which takes 1 minute. There is only one doctor, Dr. Jones, available. What is the minimum total time the three students must spend in the infirmary combined?

Charlie, Helen, and Leo plan to borrow the same picture book from the library. Charlie says he can finish reading it in one week, Helen says she needs half a month, and Leo says he can finish it in 5 days. In what order should they borrow the book to minimize the total waiting time for all three of them?

Luke, Faye, and Ben go to a barbershop together. There is only one barber available. Luke needs 20 minutes for a haircut, Faye needs 30 minutes, and Ben needs 40 minutes. In what order should they get their haircuts to minimize the total waiting time for all three of them?

Liam is on the school table tennis team. For every training session, changing clothes takes 3 minutes, changing shoes takes 2 minutes, getting his racket takes 1 minute, warming up takes 4 minutes, and listening to the sports broadcast takes 2 minutes. What is the minimum time required for Liam to start training?

In the evening, Mia needs to complete the following tasks: listening to music (10 minutes), washing up (20 minutes), and eating dinner (30 minutes). What is the minimum time required to complete these tasks?

Every morning, Lewis needs to get the following things done: making breakfast (10 minutes), eating breakfast (10 minutes), listening to the radio (20 minutes), and gardening (30 minutes). What is the minimum time required to complete these tasks?

Every morning, Lily needs to get the following things done: boiling water (10 minutes), making breakfast (20 minutes), doing laundry (20 minutes), and using the bathroom (20 minutes). What is the minimum time required to complete these tasks?

Three colleagues need to use the same printer. James needs 3 minutes to print, Kyle needs 2 minutes, and Mike needs 7 minutes. In what order should they print to minimize the total waiting time for the group?

Three athletes need to use the same running track individually. Alan needs 8 minutes, Brian needs 10 minutes, and Chris needs 12 minutes. To minimize the total waiting time for the group, in what order should they run?

Three chefs share a single stove. Chef A needs 12 minutes to finish cooking, Chef B needs 15 minutes, and Chef C needs 8 minutes. To minimize the total waiting time for the group, in what order should they cook?

Three siblings—an older brother, an older sister, and a younger sister—are using the same printer to print their documents. The brother's task takes 16 minutes, the older sister's takes 10 minutes, and the younger sister's takes 8 minutes. To minimize the total combined waiting time for all three, in what order should they arrange their printing?
examples.target: Younger sister — Older sister — Older brother

examples.input: Three surgeons need to take turns performing operations on a single operating table. Arthur requires 100 minutes to complete his surgery, Bill requires 50 minutes, and Charles requires 15 minutes. What sequence results in the minimum total waiting time?

Lisa needs to complete the following tasks every morning: washing up (10 minutes), listening to music (20 minutes), making breakfast (10 minutes), and cleaning (20 minutes). What is the minimum amount of time required for her to complete all these tasks?

Ethan plans to make a cup of hot chocolate. The process requires washing the mug (1 minute), boiling water (2 minutes), getting the cocoa powder (2 minutes), and mixing (3 minutes). If Ethan arranges the tasks efficiently to have the drink in the shortest time, how many minutes is that?

Laura is preparing boiled vegetables for dinner. The tasks involve washing the vegetables (5 minutes), boiling water (5 minutes), prepping the ingredients (10 minutes), boiling the vegetables (10 minutes), and plating (5 minutes). What is the minimum number of minutes she needs to have dinner ready?

Frank needs to take care of the following morning chores: running the washing machine (30 minutes), hanging up the laundry (10 minutes), folding clothes (10 minutes), listening to the radio (20 minutes), making breakfast (20 minutes), and taking his child to school (50 minutes). By arranging the schedule efficiently, what is the minimum total number of minutes required for him to complete all these tasks?

Iris has several tasks to handle around noon: cooking (30 minutes), listening to the radio (30 minutes), hand-washing underwear (10 minutes), running the washer (30 minutes), and picking up her child (20 minutes). If she schedules these activities efficiently, what is the minimum time required to complete them all?

Mrs. Lewis is preparing milk for a child. Her tasks involve: filling the kettle (1 minute), boiling the water (6 minutes), washing the bottle (5 minutes), getting the milk powder (2 minutes), mixing the milk (1 minute), and cooling the milk (3 minutes). If Mrs. Lewis arranges these tasks efficiently, what is the minimum number of minutes required for the child to drink the milk?

Rachel finishes school at 8:00 PM. After school, she needs to do the following tasks: doing homework (60 minutes), listening to music (30 minutes), taking a shower (40 minutes), playing video games (40 minutes), and cleaning her room (40 minutes). What is the earliest time she can go to sleep?

Uma and her mother are going out. Before leaving, they need to complete the following tasks: sweeping (5 minutes), washing clothes in the automatic washing machine (20 minutes), tidying the room (10 minutes), washing face and combing hair (8 minutes), and mopping (8 minutes). Please help them arrange the tasks efficiently and calculate how long it will take before they can set off?

Vicky is going to visit her grandmother. Before leaving, she needs to tidy the room (20 minutes), wash clothes in the automatic washing machine (30 minutes), cook (20 minutes), and pack the meal (5 minutes). If she arranges the tasks efficiently, what is the shortest time before she can leave?
If Henry has 10 hours available, and he spends 5 hours doing homework, 2 hours playing video games, 1 hour cooking, and 1 hour eating, what is the maximum amount of free time he has left?
If Lisa has 8 hours available on Friday, and she spends 3 hours doing homework, 1 hour listening to music, and 1 hour cooking, what is the maximum amount of free time she has left?

Sarah is hosting guests and needs to complete the following tasks: boiling water (10 minutes), brewing tea (5 minutes), fetching snacks (5 minutes), and arranging the snacks (10 minutes). What is the minimum number of minutes required for her to finish?

Mike needs to schedule time for the following tasks: (1) making his bed (3 minutes), (2) packing his backpack (3 minutes), (3) washing up (10 minutes), and (4) listening to a podcast (8 minutes). If he wakes up at 6:00 A.M., what is the earliest time he can finish these tasks?

Sophie wants to help her mother with chores this weekend. The tasks are: washing clothes in an automatic washing machine (20 minutes), sweeping the floor (6 minutes), wiping the tables (10 minutes), and hanging the clothes to dry (5 minutes). With efficient scheduling, what is the minimum number of minutes she needs to complete these tasks?

Tom gets up in the morning. His tasks include: brushing his teeth and washing his face (3 minutes), tidying his room (8 minutes), eating breakfast (5 minutes), and listening to a podcast (13 minutes). How many minutes does he need, at a minimum, to complete these tasks?

Adam's family has two things to do every morning: boiling milk and washing 3 milk bottles. Given that boiling milk takes 10 minutes and washing one bottle takes 3 minutes, what is the minimum time required for the family to drink the milk?

Daisy's classmate is visiting, and Daisy needs to make tea for her guest. Preparing snacks takes 3 minutes, boiling water takes 5 minutes, finding the cups takes 2 minutes, washing the cups (without using hot water) takes 1 minute, and brewing tea takes 1 minute. What is the minimum time required for Daisy to serve the tea to her classmate?

During the COVID-19 pandemic lockdown, Fiona helped her mother with housework. Sweeping the floor took 5 minutes, washing clothes in the washing machine took 40 minutes, making the bed took 3 minutes, and hanging the clothes to dry took 5 minutes. What is the minimum time required to complete these tasks?

Sarah has come down with a cold and intends to rest immediately after taking her medication. It takes 1 minute to find a cup, 6 minutes for the boiling water to reach a drinkable temperature, 1 minute to locate the cold medicine, and 5 minutes to measure her body temperature. By arranging these tasks efficiently, what is the minimum amount of time Sarah needs?

Chloe wakes up in the morning. It takes 3 minutes to rinse the rice, 20 minutes to cook the rice in a rice cooker, 10 minutes to study Spanish vocabulary, 5 minutes to brush her teeth and wash her face, and 9 minutes to eat breakfast. What is the minimum number of minutes required to complete all these tasks?

On Sunday, Lucas completed the following activities: helping his dad roast a dish in the oven (40 minutes), doing his literature homework (15 minutes), and doing his math homework (10 minutes). If he arranged his time efficiently, what is the minimum number of minutes required to complete these tasks?

Jason gets up early in the morning. It takes 8 minutes to wash his face, brush his teeth, and make his bed; 6 minutes to do morning exercises; 2 minutes to wash a mug (without using hot water) and prepare the powdered milk; and 15 minutes to boil water. Jason wants to drink his milk as soon as possible. What is the minimum number of minutes required to complete these tasks?

Sarah wakes up at 6:00 A.M. She needs to complete the following tasks: making her bed (3 minutes), brushing her teeth and washing her face (8 minutes), studying a foreign language (26 minutes), eating breakfast (10 minutes), clearing the table and cleaning up (9 minutes), and listening to the radio (30 minutes). What is the earliest time she can finish everything?

If Wendy has 10 hours available over the weekend and needs to complete the following tasks: doing math homework (1 hour), listening to music (1 hour), doing literature homework (1 hour), and cooking (1 hour). Assuming she arranges her time efficiently, what is the maximum number of hours remaining for her free time?

David, Michael, and Susan arrive at the clinic simultaneously. With only one doctor available to treat them sequentially, David needs 10 minutes for an injection, Michael needs 12 minutes for a consultation, and Susan needs 15 minutes for an injection. In what order should they be treated to minimize the total waiting time for all three patients?

If Natalie has 12 hours available on Friday to complete the following activities: tidying up the house (2 hours), listening to an audio drama (1 hour), cooking a stew (2 hours), and playing video games (1 hour). Assuming she arranges her time efficiently, what is the maximum number of hours remaining for her free time?

Alex, Ben, and Chris have each ordered a dish. With only one chef available, Alex's meal takes 10 minutes to prepare, Ben's takes 8 minutes, and Chris's takes 12 minutes. In what order should the meals be prepared to minimize the total waiting time for all three customers?

A small clinic has only one doctor on duty over the weekend, and the doctor can treat only one patient at a time. It takes 4 minutes to give Hannah an injection, 2 minutes to write a prescription for Mike, and 25 minutes to change Sarah's dressing. If the doctor schedules the patients efficiently, what is the minimum total amount of time the three patients spend waiting?
A frying pan can cook two pancakes simultaneously. It takes 3 minutes to fry one side. How many minutes are required to fully cook 5 pancakes?

Adam, Ben, and Caleb arrive at a water tap at the same time. Adam needs 15 minutes to wash bed sheets, Ben needs 10 minutes to wash clothes, and Caleb needs 2 minutes to fill a bucket. In what order should they use the tap to minimize the total waiting time?

Jessica needs to complete the following morning tasks: doing laundry in an automatic washing machine (20 minutes), watering the flowers (10 minutes), listening to the radio (10 minutes), making breakfast (30 minutes), and organizing her clothes (30 minutes). If she schedules her activities efficiently, what is the minimum amount of time required?

Ruby, Lucy, and Betty go to an ice skating rink to rent the same set of equipment. Ruby needs to rent it for 1 day, Lucy for 2 days, and Betty for half a day. What is the order that minimizes the total waiting time for the three of them?

Three skateboarders need to use the same course sequentially. Arthur takes 20 minutes, Blake takes 30 minutes, and Caleb takes 12 minutes. In what order should they skate to minimize the total waiting time for all three?

Emily wants to help her mother with housework on her Sunday off. Her tasks are: tidying the room (30 minutes), mopping the floor (30 minutes), washing bed sheets in an automatic washing machine (1 hour), hand-washing her clothes (30 minutes), and hanging the laundry to dry (10 minutes). What is the minimum number of minutes required to complete all these tasks?

Three actors are waiting to audition. Austin requires 12 minutes for his audition, Brandon requires 18 minutes, and Cameron requires 22 minutes. What is the sequence that minimizes the total waiting time for the three of them?

Noah returns home to help his grandmother. It takes 10 minutes to boil water, 30 minutes to listen to music, 60 minutes for the washing machine to wash clothes, and 30 minutes to cook. What is the minimum number of minutes required to complete all these tasks?

Chloe needs to complete the following tasks in the morning: eating breakfast (8 minutes), packing her schoolbag (2 minutes), and listening to the radio (10 minutes). What is the minimum number of minutes required to finish all of them?

Nina wakes up early in the morning. It takes 10 minutes to wash up, 10 minutes to do her morning reading, 10 minutes to boil water, 5 minutes to prepare her milk, and 5 minutes to eat breakfast. What is the minimum number of minutes required before she can leave for school?
